task_completion.visual_evidence: The Splunk Web interface is visible, indicating successful login with the provided credentials.

task_completion.description: Create the three specified event types with correct search strings
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Settings > Event types page shows 'linux_oom_event', 'linux_hardware_fault', and 'linux_disk_error' created with their respective index=system_logs search queries.

task_completion.description: Apply the 'system_failure' tag to the event types
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Settings > Tags page or the Event types configuration shows the tag 'system_failure' applied to the three newly created event types.
A Splunk dashboard titled 'Linux_Kernel_Health' is visible in the UI.

task_completion.description: Configure at least two visualization panels utilizing the 'system_failure' tag
task_completion.visual_evidence: The dashboard contains at least two visualization panels, and their underlying SPL searches (visible in edit mode or search view) include the 'system_failure' tag (e.g., tag=system_failure).

integrity.description: Agent used the Splunk Web UI to configure the settings
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with the Splunk web interface rather than terminal curl commands to the REST API.

integrity.description: The dashboard is a genuine Splunk dashboard
integrity.visual_evidence: The dashboard is viewed within the Splunk application, not as a static HTML file or mockup in a text editor.

integrity.description: Agent did not bypass the UI by directly editing Splunk .conf files
integrity.visual_evidence: No terminal windows showing the editing of savedsearches.conf, eventtypes.conf, or tags.conf using nano/vim.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Expected Dashboard Name: 'Linux_Kernel_Health'. Expected Tag: 'system_failure'. Expected Event Types: 'linux_oom_event', 'linux_hardware_fault', 'linux_disk_error'. Splunk Credentials: admin / SplunkAdmin1!
